item.title: Shipping industry casts doubt on Trump’s ‘present’ from Iran
item.description: Claim that 20 Pakistan-flagged ships will exit Strait of Hormuz questioned as country only has 13 big ocean-going vessels

item.title: Asia turns to coal as Iran war chokes off gas supplies
item.description: Countries shrug off environmental concerns to fire up use of polluting fuel as Gulf supplies dwindle

item.title: Distressed-debt funds target private credit downturn as ‘greatest opportunity’ since 2008
item.description: Investors anticipate moneymaking bonanza as sector comes under strain

item.title: Eli Lilly signs $2bn deal for AI drug development with Hong Kong biotech
item.description: Global pharmaceutical companies are aggressively searching for new medicines in China

item.title: Burford’s court loss shows risk in listed litigation funders
item.description: A volatile business often involving binary outcomes may not be suited for risk-averse public market investors

item.title: Fermi shares plunge on $486mn net loss
item.description: Data centre property group faces investor concerns around lack of tenant revenue

item.title: UK to receive last tanker of jet fuel from Middle East this week
item.description: Industry warnings of disruption contrast with government calls for calm

item.title: Solicitors regulator to investigate fewer cases after complaints rise
item.description: Chief executive says fees will also have to rise to address growing demands

item.title: Air Canada CEO to step down over failure to speak French
item.description: Michael Rousseau recorded condolence video after fatal plane crash in English despite country being officially bi-lingual

item.title: You can’t blame AI for mistakes, regulator tells auditors
item.description: Financial Reporting Council issues guidance stressing need for human oversight

item.title: UK regulator cuts £2bn from car finance redress costs for banks
item.description: Financial Conduct Authority lowers estimate for loans eligible for compensation scheme

item.title: Qatar-backed US LNG plant starts production as Iran war hits global supply
item.description: Golden Pass plant owned by QatarEnergy and ExxonMobil may help replace shortages hit by Hormuz crisis

item.title: Delaware judge reassigns Musk cases after LinkedIn row
item.description: Chancellor Kathaleen McCormick denies bias but cites media glare as risk to justice

item.title: ECB warns Monte dei Paschi over chief executive succession battle
item.description: Board’s proposed new leader Fabrizio Palermo may not meet regulator’s approval

item.title: UK government on verge of full nationalisation of British Steel
item.description: Talks with Chinese owner Jingye continue while losses mount

item.title: Iranian strike on Kuwaiti power and water plant stokes infrastructure fears
item.description: Attack at electricity and desalination facility marks latest escalation in Gulf war launched by US and Israel

item.title: A blueprint for Chinese global leadership
item.description: With the US destroying its own credibility, the opportunity is Beijing’s for the taking

item.title: Trump to take first steps in opening retirement funds to private markets
item.description: US Department of Labor to offer plan administrators safe harbour process for selecting alternative investments

item.title: BP loses head of EV charging as it accelerates pivot back to oil and gas
item.description: Martin Thomsen’s departure comes ahead of arrival of oil major’s new chief executive this week

item.title: Private credit: how worried should we be?
item.description: Join FT journalists for a subscriber webinar on April 16

item.title: Can Europe make a difference?
item.description: The backlash against Big Tech in America might be an opportunity for the EU

item.title: Sirius Real Estate strikes Germany deal as it targets €1bn in defence investments
item.description: FTSE 250 group to acquire site mostly let to Rheinmetall for almost €100mn

item.title: And the FTAV charts quiz winner is . . .
item.description: ChatGPTee?

item.title: Iran shocks make their way inexorably to the grocery aisle
item.description: Fertiliser is another casualty of the closed Strait of Hormuz

item.title: AI satellite start-ups gain traction with investors ahead of SpaceX IPO
item.description: Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos’s commitment to putting data centres in space boosts fortunes of smaller players

item.title: FirstFT: Trump says US could ‘take’ Iran oil
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: European AI developer Mistral raises $830mn in debut debt sale and WTO talks end in acrimony

item.title: Need to be extracted from the Middle East? Call your wealth manager
item.description: The rich are increasingly relying on financial advisers to get them out of harm’s way during the Iran war

item.title: UK fines Apple subsidiary for breaching Russian sanctions
item.description: European operation ordered to pay £390,000 for payments to video-streaming service Okko

item.title: Mistral raises $830mn to build Nvidia-powered AI centres in Europe
item.description: French company’s debut debt financing follows rising demand for alternatives to US groups

item.title: Short rates have overshot
item.description: Plus the social media lawsuits
